posts.title: Building Paani Boss: From Idea to Play Store in 6 Weeks
posts.excerpt: How I shipped a complete RO water service management app with field tracking, AMC scheduling and billing using Flutter and Supabase.
posts.author: RAOGY
posts.content:
# Building Paani Boss

When a local RO water service owner complained about juggling pen-and-paper customer records, manual billing, and missed AMC renewals, I saw an opportunity.

## The Problem

Small RO water service businesses in India run on chaos:

- **Customer data** scattered across notebooks
- **AMC renewals** tracked mentally (and often missed)
- **Field technicians** with no route planning
- **Billing** done in cash with handwritten receipts

## The Stack

| Layer | Tech |
|-------|------|
| App | Flutter (cross-platform iOS + Android) |
| Backend | Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage) |
| Realtime | Supabase Realtime |
| Maps | Google Maps + Geolocator |

## Key Features Shipped

1. Customer CRM with searchable history
2. AMC auto-reminder via scheduled push notifications
3. GPS field tracking for technicians
4. One-tap invoice generation with WhatsApp share
5. Owner dashboard with revenue charts

## Lessons Learned

- **Offline-first is non-negotiable** for field apps in India. Hive cache saved us when reps drove into low-signal areas.
- **Supabase Row Level Security** eliminated hundreds of lines of auth code.
- **Keep the UI painfully simple**. First version had tabs, second version scrapped them for a single scrolling feed. Daily active usage doubled.

## What's Next

v2 will add technician performance scoring and customer self-service chat. Check out the [app page](/paaniboss/) for more details.

posts.title: Why Flutter Still Wins for Indie Mobile Developers in 2026
posts.excerpt: After shipping four apps across iOS and Android with a solo team, here's my honest take on Flutter vs native in 2026.
posts.author: RAOGY
posts.content:
# Why Flutter Still Wins

There's a fresh wave of native hype — Kotlin Multiplatform is maturing, SwiftUI is better than ever, and AI-generated UI code is tempting.

But for a **solo developer shipping real products**, Flutter still takes the cake in 2026. Here's why.

## 1. One Codebase, Real Parity

I ship **Paani Boss** and **TagGlo** from a single Flutter codebase and both run identically on iOS and Android. Zero platform-specific bugs, zero re-implementation.

## 2. The Widget System Ages Well

Four years in, my 2022 widgets still compile, still render pixel-perfect. Meanwhile, my UIKit code from 2021 needed 3 rewrites.

## 3. Supabase + Flutter = Ship Speed

Paired with Supabase, I can go from idea to App Store in 6 weeks:

```dart
final customers = await supabase
  .from('customers')
  .select()
  .order('created_at');
```

That's it. No backend boilerplate.

## Where Flutter Loses

Be honest — it's not perfect:

- **iOS polish** still lags Swift for ultra-premium apps
- **Build sizes** are larger than native
- **Platform channels** for obscure APIs get painful

## Verdict

For the **business value per hour** ratio, nothing beats Flutter in 2026. Your mileage may vary, but four shipped apps later, I'm still all in.

posts.title: The Supabase RLS Cheatsheet I Wish I Had Earlier
posts.excerpt: Row Level Security is Supabase's killer feature but the docs assume you know Postgres. Here's the practical cheatsheet for app devs.
posts.author: RAOGY
posts.content:
# Supabase RLS Cheatsheet

Row Level Security (RLS) is what lets you expose Supabase directly to your app without writing a backend. But the first time I set it up, I locked myself out for two hours.

## The Mental Model

Think of RLS as a bouncer at every row:

> "Hey `auth.uid()`, can you read / write this row? Show me the policy."

No policy → no access. Always enable RLS first.

## Pattern 1: Users Own Their Rows

```sql
alter table posts enable row level security;

create policy "Users can read own posts"
  on posts for select
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

create policy "Users can insert own posts"
  on posts for insert
  with check (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

## Pattern 2: Public Read, Auth Write

Perfect for blogs and comments:

```sql
create policy "Anyone can read"
  on posts for select using (true);

create policy "Only owner can update"
  on posts for update
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

## Pattern 3: Team-based Access

```sql
create policy "Team members can read team data"
  on invoices for select
  using (
    auth.uid() in (
      select user_id from team_members
      where team_id = invoices.team_id
    )
  );
```

## Debugging Tips

- Use the **SQL Editor's "Run as authenticated user"** — saved me hours
- `select auth.uid();` to verify JWT is decoded
- Check `auth.role()` to distinguish `anon`, `authenticated`, `service_role`

## Gotchas

1. Service role bypasses RLS — never expose it client-side
2. `insert` policies need `with check`, not `using`
3. Storage has its own policies (different table)

Bookmark this. You'll need it.

posts.title: I Built a Control Panel for My Own Blog in One Evening
posts.excerpt: Meet control.raogy.com — a tiny admin dashboard that lets me publish articles, swap featured images, and update live copy without touching a Git commit.
posts.author: RAOGY
posts.content:
# Meet the Control Panel

Every static-site developer eventually hits this wall: you want to publish a quick post but your blog needs a `git commit` + `npm run build` + `git push`. That friction kills momentum.

So I built **control.raogy.com**.

## What It Does

- Create, edit, delete posts
- Swap cover images (or pick a gradient)
- Preview before publishing
- Toggle draft / published
- Works from my phone

## The Stack

Two modes baked in:

1. **Local mode** (default) — edits live in `localStorage`, export/import JSON anytime. Zero setup.
2. **Cloud mode** — plug in your Supabase URL + anon key and it's real-time across devices.

## What's Next

- Markdown paste from clipboard with auto-image upload
- AI-assisted draft summaries
- Analytics tiny-graphs right on the dashboard

If you want the source, ping me.
